OBS-001.pattern: Dome light texture file parameter is xn__inputstexturefile_r3ah — not guessable from convention. Requires execute_python discovery.
OBS-001.context: Setting HDRI on dome light LOP in Houdini 21

OBS-002.pattern: rectlight is not a valid Houdini LOP node type. Available light types: distantlight, domelight, light, geometrylight, portallight, karmaskydomelight. Use 'light' for area/rect lights.
OBS-002.context: Creating rect/area lights in Solaris LOPs

OBS-003.pattern: create_material returns material_usd_path that doesn't match actual USD prim path. Returned: /materials/forge_red_mtl, actual: /materials/forge_red_mtl_shader. Always traverse stage to verify actual path.
OBS-003.context: Creating MaterialX materials via create_material MCP tool

OBS-004.pattern: read_material returns empty shader_params and shader_type for MaterialX materials. Cannot introspect MaterialX shader properties via MCP tool.

OBS-005.pattern: houdini_render on a karma LOP node hits 'permission error' (locked HDA). Must create a usdrender ROP in /out with loppath pointing to the karma LOP, then render via the ROP.
OBS-005.context: Rendering via Karma in Solaris pipeline

OBS-006.pattern: karmarenderer is not a valid LOP type name. Correct type is 'karma'. Also: karmarendersettings, karmarenderproperties, karmarenderproducts are valid.
OBS-006.context: Creating Karma render chain in Solaris

OBS-007.pattern: Light color parameters are split into individual R/G/B components: xn__inputscolor_ztar (R), xn__inputscolor_ztag (G), xn__inputscolor_ztab (B). No tuple set available via set_parm — requires 3 separate calls.
OBS-007.context: Setting light color on any USD light in Solaris

OBS-008.pattern: execute_python with atomic=true (default) prevents hou.undos.performUndo() inside the code block — 'Cannot undo within an undo group' error. Must pass atomic=false for any code that calls undo/redo. Also breaks auto-rollback on NameError/TypeError — orphan nodes persist.
OBS-008.context: Using undo system via execute_python MCP tool

OBS-009.pattern: No dedicated undo/redo MCP tool exists. Must use execute_python with atomic=false and call hou.undos.performUndo()/performRedo() manually.

OBS-010.pattern: synapse_search finds memories by keyword, synapse_recall uses a different matching algorithm and may return 0 results for the same content. memory_query with scope=scene misses entries written via add_memory (different storage scope). memory_query scope=all can timeout.

OBS-011.pattern: USD light parameter naming convention: xn__inputs{PropertyName}_{hash}. Common mappings: intensity=xn__inputsintensity_i0a, exposure=xn__inputsexposure_vya, color=xn__inputscolor_zta{r|g|b}, colorTemperature=xn__inputscolorTemperature_wcb, enableColorTemperature=xn__inputsenableColorTemperature_omb, textureFile=xn__inputstexturefile_r3ah (dome only).
OBS-011.context: Working with any USD light parameters in Houdini 21 Solaris

OBS-012.pattern: synapse_batch fails with 'undos object has no attribute beginGroup' when atomic=true. Batch command undo grouping is broken — must use individual tool calls instead.
OBS-012.context: Using synapse_batch to set multiple parameters atomically

OBS-013.pattern: houdini_render returns image via MCP but EXR file is not written to disk even when picture and outputimage parms are set. Root cause: soho_foreground=0 (default) causes rop.render() to return before husk finishes writing. Must set soho_foreground=1 for synchronous rendering that guarantees file output.
OBS-013.context: Rendering and capturing output files for validation

OBS-014.pattern: execute_python rollback fails when atomic=true (default). NameError/TypeError triggers auto-undo but hits 'Cannot undo within an undo group'. Nodes created before the error persist as orphans. Rollback only works with atomic=false.
OBS-014.context: Error recovery and scene safety during execute_python failures

OBS-015.pattern: render_settings tool correctly round-trips all parameter types: resolution (int), samples (int), pixel filter (string menu), AOV toggles (bool). No friction on settings read/write — only on output file persistence.
OBS-015.context: Karma render settings configuration via MCP

OBS-016.pattern: LOP nodes have no setRenderFlag() method — only setDisplayFlag(). Calling setRenderFlag() raises AttributeError. Display flag is sufficient for karma rendering.
OBS-016.context: Setting up karma render chain in LOPs

OBS-017.pattern: Error messages from SYNAPSE follow coaching tone consistently: 'Couldn't find parameter', 'Couldn't find a node at', 'The attempted operation failed'. All errors are recoverable — subsequent operations work normally after any error.
OBS-017.context: Error handling and recovery across all MCP tools

OBS-018.pattern: assetreference LOP nodes produce geometry visible in viewport (OpenGL) but invisible to Karma renderer. Using sublayer LOP for the same USD file renders correctly. This is a critical distinction for any automated scene assembly workflow.
OBS-018.context: Loading USD assets for Karma rendering in Houdini 21 Solaris

OBS-019.pattern: create_material handler's stage query (Fix #13) silently falls back to hardcoded path. matlib.stage() returns None or the try/except catches an error. Material USD paths end with _shader suffix (e.g., /materials/red_plastic_shader not /materials/red_plastic). Must traverse stage after creation to get real path.
OBS-019.context: Creating materials via create_material MCP tool and assigning to geometry

OBS-020.pattern: float('nan') and float('inf') values set via hou.Parm.set() persist without rejection. Houdini does not guard against these values. set_parm MCP tool should validate against NaN/Inf before setting.
OBS-020.context: Parametric edge case testing on USD light parameters

OBS-021.pattern: set_parm accepts any value for intensity parameters including values >1.0, negative, and extreme values. No Lighting Law warning emitted. Intensity should always be 1.0 — brightness via exposure only.
OBS-021.context: Setting light intensity via MCP tools

OBS-022.pattern: Variables defined at execute_python script top level are not accessible inside nested function definitions (def helpers). Houdini's embedded Python execution uses restricted globals/locals propagation. Workaround: use inline code without helper functions, or pass variables as function arguments explicitly.
OBS-022.context: Writing complex scripts via execute_python MCP tool

OBS-023.pattern: usdrender ROP with soho_foreground=0 (default) returns from rop.render() before husk finishes writing the EXR. Output file either doesn't exist or is 0 bytes. Must set soho_foreground=1 for synchronous rendering that guarantees file output. Warning: soho_foreground=1 blocks the Houdini main thread.
OBS-023.context: Rendering and validating output files via usdrender ROP
